channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Cuts Domestic Fuel Prices as Iran War Impacts Energy Markets
item.description: China announced it will lower retail price caps on gasoline and diesel from June 5, marking the second cut since the Iran war began and reflecting constrained global energy supplies. [Energy]

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel Says Lebanon Operations to Continue Despite Ceasefire Announcement
item.description: Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz stated that military operations in Lebanon will continue and troops will not withdraw, despite the announcement of a renewed ceasefire agreement. [Middle East]

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan to Expand Anti-Ship Missile Arsenal to Counter China Threat
item.description: Taiwan plans to increase its anti-ship missile arsenal to over 1,800 by early 2029, enhancing capabilities to counter potential blockade or invasion threats from China. [US-China]

item.title: 🇳🇿 China Bans Four New Zealand Lawmakers Over Taiwan Visit
item.description: Beijing barred four New Zealand MPs from entering China, Hong Kong, and Macau for one year as punishment for visiting Taiwan, prompting Wellington to express concern to Beijing. [Diplomacy]

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Tells NATO Allies to Boost Air and Naval Forces as Washington Steps Back
item.description: A top US general said Washington expects European NATO allies and Canada to swiftly increase aircraft and naval contributions to alliance defense plans as the US reduces its role in these areas. [Europe]

item.title: 🇺🇸 US House Votes to End Iran War in Rebuke to Trump
item.description: The House passed a war powers resolution 215-208 to end US involvement in the Iran war, with four Republicans joining Democrats in a rare congressional challenge to presidential military authority. [US-Iran War]

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Opens Membership Talks with Ukraine and Moldova Despite Hungarian Resistance
item.description: The European Union formally launched accession negotiations with Ukraine and Moldova, advancing their path to membership despite ongoing opposition from Hungary. [Europe]

item.title: 🇮🇩 Indonesia Arrests Deputy Immigration Minister on Graft Charges
item.description: Indonesian authorities arrested the deputy immigration minister as part of a broader crackdown on corruption within the government. [Asia-Pacific]

item.title: 🇸🇴 Somalia: Gunfire During Mogadishu Protests
item.description: Gunfire erupted during protests in Somalia's capital Mogadishu amid rising political tensions in the country. [Africa]

item.title: 🇴🇲 Oman Resists US Pressure to Break Ties with Iran Over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Oman is resisting US pressure to sever diplomatic ties with Iran, seeking to maintain its role as a neutral mediator in the Gulf amid the ongoing conflict. [Middle East]

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan Faces Naphtha Shortage Impacting Plastic Production
item.description: Japan is experiencing a naphtha shortage linked to Middle East disruptions, affecting plastic bag production and highlighting supply chain vulnerabilities. [Asia-Pacific]

item.title: 🇨🇳 China's Great Power Diplomacy: Between Trump and Putin
item.description: Analysis of how China's diplomatic strategy positions itself between the Trump administration and Putin's Russia in the evolving great power competition. [US-China]

item.title: 🇦🇫 Taliban and Russia Deepen Ties Amid Western Withdrawal
item.description: The Taliban and Russia are strengthening diplomatic and economic ties, marking a significant shift in regional power dynamics following the Western withdrawal from Afghanistan. [South Asia]

item.title: 🇸🇦 Saudi Arabia and Russia Tighten Grip on Oil Market as UAE Exits OPEC
item.description: Saudi Arabia and Russia are consolidating control over global oil markets while the UAE's departure from OPEC reshapes the energy cartel's influence. [Energy]

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Uses Job Platforms to Target Security Personnel, US Allies Warn
item.description: US allies are warning that China is using job recruitment platforms to target security personnel in Western countries for intelligence gathering. [Cyber]

item.title: 🇮🇱 Trump Called Netanyahu 'Crazy' in Phone Call About Iran Ceasefire
item.description: President Trump criticized Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu as "crazy" during a phone call discussing the Iran ceasefire, revealing tensions between the allies over war strategy. [Middle East]

item.title: 🇬🇷 Greece Issues Formal Protest Over Ukraine Naval Drone Incident
item.description: Greece formally protested to Ukraine over a naval drone incident, highlighting diplomatic tensions as maritime warfare continues in the Black Sea region. [Ukraine-Russia War]

item.title: 🇺🇦 US House Advances Ukraine Aid Bill, Setting Up Final Vote
item.description: The US House of Representatives advanced a Ukraine aid bill, moving it closer to a final vote that could authorize additional military and economic support. [Ukraine-Russia War]

item.title: 🇭🇺 Hungary Could Host Ukraine-Russia Peace Talks, Orban Says
item.description: Hungary has offered to potentially host peace negotiations between Ukraine and Russia, according to statements from Hungarian officials. [Diplomacy]

item.title: 🇺🇦 NATO Chief Arrives in Kyiv After Mass Russian Strike
item.description: NATO's Secretary General arrived in Kyiv one day after a massive Russian missile strike, demonstrating continued alliance support for Ukraine. [Ukraine-Russia War]

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran War Day 97: Tehran Says No Progress in Talks; Israel Attacks Lebanon
item.description: On day 97 of the Iran war, Tehran reports no progress in negotiations while Israel continues drone attacks on Lebanon despite ceasefire discussions. [US-Iran War]

item.title: 📉 Oil Falls as Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire Boosts Hopes for Broader Agreement
item.description: Oil prices declined as a ceasefire deal between Israel and Lebanon raised hopes for a broader agreement to end the US-Iran conflict and potentially reopen the Strait of Hormuz. [Energy]

item.title: ⚔️ War May End with Interim Deal Leaving Iran Battered But Unbroken
item.description: Analysis suggests the US-Iran war could conclude with an interim agreement that leaves Iran damaged but not defeated, reshaping regional power dynamics. [US-Iran War]

item.title: 🏠 Russian Attack Kills Three Civilians in Ukraine's Kramatorsk
item.description: Russian shelling killed at least three civilians in Kramatorsk, while Moscow's forces attacked areas near Dnipro with drones and missiles, Ukrainian officials said. [Ukraine-Russia War]

item.title: ⚽ FIFA Bans Reusable Water Bottles at World Cup Venues
item.description: FIFA announced attendees cannot bring reusable water bottles into World Cup venues due to safety concerns, following a last-minute policy change. [Geopolitics]

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Bans New Zealand MPs from Entering After Taiwan Visit
item.description: China barred four New Zealand lawmakers from entering Hong Kong, Macau, and the Chinese mainland for a year as punishment for visiting Taiwan, prompting diplomatic concerns. [Diplomacy]
